channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Groups suspend use of 'suicide capsule' pending criminal probe of US woman's death
item.description: Swiss police arrested several people following the death of an unidentified 64-year-old woman from the U.S. Midwest who on Sept. 23 became the first person to use the device.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: VIDEO: Historic satellite imagery shows three active hurricanes in Atlantic Basin
item.description: For the first time, three hurricanes are simultaneously active in the Atlantic Basin in October, according to the Cooperative Institute for Research in the Atmosphere (CIRA).
item.dc:creator: Garrett Phillips, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Can you stop calls and texts from political campaigns?
item.description: If you've received a political text or call recently, you may be wondering how to make it stop, or what you can do to prevent them in the future.
item.dc:creator: Samantha Jarpe, ABC 4

item.title: Voters in North Carolina and Georgia have bigger problems than politics. Helene changed everything
item.description: VILAS, N.C. (AP) — Brad Farrington pulls over to grab a case of water bottles being passed out in Vilas, a small rural community tucked away in the Blue Ridge Mountains. He’s on his way to help a friend who lost much of what he owned when Hurricane Helene blew through last weekend.
item.dc:creator: MAKIYA SEMINERA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Here's what Spirit Halloween seeks in an 'ideal' retail location
item.description: Spirit Halloween made headlines earlier this week after firing back at "SNL" over a sketch that mocked the company's choice of retail locations.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: See the 10 most – and least – valuable college majors: study
item.description: A new study from financial publisher Bankrate found that STEM college majors dominate the ranking of valuable fields of study – but money isn't the only consideration.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Fact-checking scam claims about Hurricane Helene
item.description: Scam artists, bad-faith actors and those with political agendas are spreading falsehoods, says a White House statement.
item.dc:creator: Rich Johnson, NewsNation

item.title: Death toll from Hurricane Helene rises to 227 as grim task of recovering bodies continues
item.description: FRANKFORT, Ky. (AP) — The death toll from Hurricane Helene inched up to 227 on Saturday as the grim task of recovering bodies continued more than a week after the monster storm ravaged the Southeast and killed people in six states. Helene came ashore Sept.
item.dc:creator: BRUCE SCHREINER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Elon Musk makes appearance at Trump rally casting election in dire terms
item.description: (AP) - Billionaire tech executive Elon Musk cast the upcoming presidential election in dire terms during a Saturday appearance with Donald Trump, calling the Republican presidential nominee the only candidate “to preserve democracy in America.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: 30-foot wall of water hit Chimney Rock, firefighter recalls; officials refute 'bodies everywhere' rumor
item.description: Chimney Rock volunteer firefighter John Payne saw what he estimated was a 30-foot-high wall of water, tossing car-sized boulders as it raced toward the town.
item.dc:creator: ALLEN G. BREED Associate Press, NewsNation

item.title: Former New York governor and stepson assaulted during evening walk
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Former New York Gov. David Paterson and his stepson sustained minor injuries when they were assaulted on a Manhattan street by four men and a woman as they went for an evening walk, police said Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Child entrepreneur opens pizza shop with Caribbean twist in New York City
item.description: A young entrepreneur is bringing even more Caribbean flavor to New York City, opening a pizza shop with a twist at 11 years old.
item.dc:creator: Zhané Caldwell, NewsNation

item.title: These cities have the worst drivers in the U.S., study says
item.description: A Tennessee city has once again been given the dubious title of home to the worst drivers in the United States.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, NewsNation

item.title: Entire Chicago school board to resign over teachers union dispute with Dem mayor: 'Deeply alarming'
item.description: The entire Chicago Board of Education is resigning after reportedly declining to fire the Chicago Public Schools CEO during contract negotiations with the city's teachers union.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Where do Trump, Harris stand on marijuana policy?
item.description: In a poll taken last year, roughly 70% of American adults said marijuana should be legal — but where do the presidential candidates stand?
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump's return to Butler, Pennsylvania, site of first assassination attempt, is 'gutsy,' supporters say
item.description: Supporters praised former President Trump for returning to Butler, Pennsylvania following the July assassination attempt, saying that it was "courageous" and "gutsy.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: New Jersey sisters battle stage 4 colon cancer together
item.description: They share a lot more than their sister bond. They are both in their late 30s and currently battling stage four colon cancer.
item.dc:creator: Grace Gomez, KRON4

item.title: After the deluge, the lies: Misinformation and hoaxes about Helene cloud the recovery
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The facts emerging from Hurricane Helene's destruction are heartrending: Businesses and homes destroyed, whole communities nearly wiped out, hundreds of lives lost, hundreds of people missing.
item.dc:creator: DAVID KLEPPER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Does a cumulus cloud really weigh a million pounds?
item.description: Just because something floats doesn't mean it is weightless. A great example is when oil is poured onto water. The oil will float on top of the water, but that doesn't mean it has no weight.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Bacca, Fox 5

item.title: Homeowners hit by Hurricane Helene face the grim task of rebuilding without flood insurance
item.description: A week after Hurricane Helene overwhelmed the Southeastern U.S., homeowners hit the hardest are grappling with how they could possibly pay for the flood damage from one of the deadliest storms to hit the mainland in recent history.
item.dc:creator: SALLY HO, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Many loathe public speakerphone calls, so why do people do it?
item.description: Few things are as universally reviled as a loud phone conversation infringing on your personal space. (Mosquitoes and taxes, aside.) Doing it on loudspeaker is even more incendiary.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, KTLA 5

item.title: Air Force cadet's cause of death blamed on rare bacterial infection
item.description: Avery Koonce, a 19-year-old Air Force Academy cadet who was found dead in her dorm room on Sept. 4, died from a rare bacterial infection, a coroner says.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Dallas Uber driver seen on video punching passenger after dispute over legroom
item.description: A 68-year-old Uber passenger was punched in the back of the head in Dallas, Texas, after he complained about the lack of legroom inside a car, a report says.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Idaho state senator tells Native American candidate 'go back where you came from' in forum
item.description: KENDRICK, Idaho (AP) — Tensions rose during a bipartisan forum this week after an audience question about discrimination reportedly led an Idaho state senator to angrily tell a Native American candidate to “go back where you came from.” Republican Sen.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Judge denies an order sought by a Black student who was punished over his hair
item.description: HOUSTON (AP) — A federal judge on Friday denied a request by a Black high school student in Texas for a court order that the student’s lawyers say would have allowed him to return to his high school without fear of having his previous punishment over his hairstyle resume.
item.dc:creator: JUAN A. LOZANO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Dolly Parton, Walmart announce millions in donations to Helene victims
item.description: East Tennessee's own Dolly Parton is expected to offer her assistance to help with the flood recovery efforts in Newport.
item.dc:creator: Hope McAlee, Fox 5

item.title: Ohio woman's viral TikTok videos lead prompt search for body in her yard
item.description: Police in Columbus, Ohio have concluded an investigation after a woman posted on TikTok that she might have discovered a body buried in her yard.
item.dc:creator: Katie Millard, Fox 5

item.title: TSA detects shotgun shell containing meth hidden in traveler's groin area at Pennsylvania airport
item.description: A traveler attempting to hide a prohibited substance inside a prohibited item — and then tucking them into his groin area — was quickly caught by TSA officers this week.
item.dc:creator: Hayden Sherry, ABC 4

item.title: For Pittsburgh Jews, attack anniversary adds to an already grim October
item.description: PITTSBURGH (AP) — Jewish communities everywhere reacted with horror at last year's Oct. 7 attack by Hamas on Israel, but the approaching one-year commemoration of the assault hits home particularly hard in Pittsburgh's Jewish community, which already marks a grim anniversary each October.
item.dc:creator: PETER SMITH, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Biden student loan cancellation plan on hold again
item.description: The Biden administration’s student loan cancellation plan has been put on hold once again in Missouri after brief celebration from advocates this week when another judge ruled the plan could move forward.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, WGN 9

item.title: Los Angeles D.A. to ‘reevaluate’ Menendez brothers’ convictions
item.description: Los Angeles County District Attorney George Gascón provided an update Thursday on the convictions of Erik and Lyle Menendez, who were convicted of killing their parents in August 1989.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Garth Brooks responds to sexual assault allegations
item.description: Country music superstar Garth Brooks has issued a statement following a lawsuit accusing him of raping a hair and makeup artist in 2019.
item.dc:creator: Brittney Baird, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Mixed verdict for 3 Memphis officers convicted in Tyre Nichols' fatal beating
item.description: MEMPHIS, Tenn. (AP) — Three former Memphis police officers were convicted Thursday in the 2023 fatal beating of Tyre Nichols, but were acquitted of the harshest charges they faced for a death that sparked national protests and calls for broad changes in policing.
item.dc:creator: ADRIAN SAINZ, KRISTIN M. HALL and JONATHAN MATTISE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Los Angeles prosecutors to review new evidence in Menendez brothers' 1996 murder conviction
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Prosecutors in Los Angeles are reviewing new evidence in the case of Erik and Lyle Menendez to determine whether they should be serving life sentences for killing their parents in their Beverly Hills mansion more than 35 years ago, the city's district attorney said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTOPHER WEBER and JAIMIE DING, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Subway rider shot in the head by police files claim accusing officers of recklessly opening fire
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A lawyer for a New York City subway rider who was inadvertently shot in the head by police at a station has filed a legal claim against the city, accusing the officers of showing “carelessness and reckless” disregard for the lives of others when they opened fire on a man holding
item.dc:creator: CEDAR ATTANASIO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Hurricanes like Helene are deadly when they strike and keep killing for years to come
item.description: Hurricanes in the United States end up hundreds of times deadlier than the government calculates, contributing to more American deaths than car accidents or all the nation's wars, a new study said. The average storm hitting the U.S.
item.dc:creator: SETH BORENSTEIN, Associated Press, ABC 4
